title: stevia elatior var. typica
intro: Stevia elatior var. typica, also known as Stevia rebaudiana, is a plant species native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Paraguay and Brazil, and is a member of the Asteraceae family. Stevia elatior var. typica is a perennial plant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant's leaves contain compounds such as stevioside and rebaudioside, which provide numerous health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving blood sugar control, and promoting oral health. Stevia elatior var. typica can be consumed as a dietary supplement, and its leaves are also used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
